mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 60-day return in at least 50% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth, with forward returns reverting to random walk behavior.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth rates signal strengthening economic momentum, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and higher S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and inflation expectations indicates real rate compression, which boosts equity valuations and generates positive returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 per month during high unemployment periods signals aggressive labor market recovery, driving positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 200k fails to predict positive 20-day returns when UNRATE remains above 5.5%, with accuracy dropping below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production greater than 0.5% indicates sudden supply-side contraction, prompting immediate market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 records positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases following an INDPRO drop exceeding 0.5%.
